markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shenzhen be 27°C or below on June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from agencies like the China Meteorological Administration show Shenzhen's June 5 high likely peaking near 34–36°C amid typical early-summer subtropical conditions, with high humidity, southeasterly monsoon flow, and scattered thunderstorms that can cap daytime heating. The tight clustering of market-implied odds around 34–36°C reflects genuine uncertainty in the exact timing and coverage of cloud build-up and rainfall, which directly influences peak surface temperatures before any cooling from convection. Official observations will resolve the market based on the daily maximum recorded at Shenzhen's primary station, consistent with historical June baselines near 31°C but elevated by current warm advection.
